As the curtains slowly drew back to reveal the interview room, I felt a sudden sense of unease. The room was dimly lit, and I could hear the buzz of the air conditioner and the hum of the lights. The atmosphere was tense, and I could feel the other candidates' nervousness radiating off them.

But then, I caught a glimpse of the interviewer, and something in my gut told me to relax. She was a petite woman, with curly brown hair and a friendly smile that seemed to extend to the other candidates as well. She introduced herself as the hiring manager, and my heart began to race as I took a deep breath and faced her.

As the interview got underway, I found myself feeling a mix of nerves and excitement. I had applied for the job months ago, and it was finally happening. However, what I didn't expect was the sudden sense of surprise that came over me as soon as the interviewer asked me to share a personal story.
I tried to hide my embarrassment, but my face turned as red as a tomato. My heart was racing, and my stomach was churning. I felt like I had stepped into a nightmare, and I wasn't sure if I was ready for this.
The interviewer noticed my tension and took the opportunity to ask me to share something more personal.
